summary: Redesign scoreFinalQualityAutomated: add test coverage depth (0-15) and API surface consistency (0-15), rebalance weights
context: finalQuality scored 89-93 across all conditions (only 4pt spread) because binary compile/pass checks dominated. Every condition satisfies these, so no discriminating signal.
rationale: Six-component scorer (total 100): (1) Compilation 0-15 (was 0-20), (2) Test pass rate 0-20 (was 0-30), (3) Component completeness 0-20 (was 0-25), (4) Architecture consistency 0-15 (unchanged), (5) NEW test coverage depth 0-15 (test files per component across 7 patterns), (6) NEW API surface consistency 0-15 (file naming conventions, exported types, consistent adapter send() method). Components 5 and 6 are differentiating: coordinated agents writing test files for each component and following consistent naming conventions will score higher than agents that only compile and pass tests.

alternatives.option: Keep 5-component scorer with adjusted weights only
alternatives.reason_rejected: Reducing binary check weights increases spread slightly but the fundamental problem is that all conditions produce working code — need dimensions that reward depth beyond working.
Adds cost and non-determinism per run. Per decision 01KN8YSGRSVY8E19P02PWZYSQ8, automated scorer can discriminate with richer sub-dimensions.

assumptions: Coordinated agents will write more test files per component than baseline agents
Coordinated agents will follow consistent file naming (*.service.ts, *.adapter.ts) more reliably
The 7 componentTestPatterns cover realistic test file names an agent would create
